name: Trans-Cantabrian Trail
name_original: Peña Ciquera desde el collado de Piedrasluengas — trailrunning por el Parque Natural Montaña Palentina
description: This difficult 5.4-mile trail running route to Peña Ciquera offers panoramic views and a challenging climb in Parque Natural Montaña Palenti
region_intro: Jogging around La Pernía offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its location within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. The region features varied terrain, including river valleys like the Pisuerga, forested areas, and routes leading to significant peaks such as Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares. Trails often navigate through natural woodlands and past geological formations, providing a mix of challenging ascents and scenic paths. Best jogging routes around La Pernía The most popular jogging route is Nacimiento del río Pisuerga por la Senda de Fuente Cobre, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the source of the Pisuerga River within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Peña Labra y Pico Tres Mares, a difficult 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This route offers a challenging trail run from Fuente del Chivo, ascending towards prominent peaks. Local runners also love the Senda "El Roblón de Estalaya", a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, often completed in about 31 minutes. Jogging around La Pernía is defined by its river valleys, natural park woodlands, and mountain ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to more difficult trail running challenges. The routes in La Pernía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore La Pernía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in La Pernía?
A: La Pernía offers a good selection of running routes, with over 15 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from moderate forest paths to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in La Pernía?
A: The jogging routes in La Pernía are characterized by diverse terrain within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. You'll find river valleys, forested areas, and paths leading to significant peaks like Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares. Expect a mix of natural woodlands, geological formations, and both challenging ascents and scenic, winding paths.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in La Pernía?
A: Yes, La Pernía features several circular running routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120143103/nacimiento-del-rio-pisuerga-por-la-senda-de-fuente-cobre-pr-p-8-circular-trailrunning-por-el-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Pisuerga por la Senda de Fuente Cobre&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the source of the Pisuerga River. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120138161/senda-el-roblon-de-estalaya-prc-p-4-circular-trailrunning-por-el-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda "El Roblón de Estalaya"&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate circular trail through the natural park.

Q: Are there options for beginners or easy runs in La Pernía?
A: While many routes in La Pernía are rated as difficult, there are moderate options suitable for those looking for less strenuous runs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120138161/senda-el-roblon-de-estalaya-prc-p-4-circular-trailrunning-por-el-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda "El Roblón de Estalaya"&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a moderate trail, offering a pleasant run through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park.

Q: What are some points of interest or natural attractions I might see while running?
A: La Pernía is rich in natural beauty. Along your runs, you might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3837834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pisuerga River Source (Cueva del Cobre)&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt;, or the panoramic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region is part of a natural park, so scenic vistas are abundant.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in La Pernía?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Please respect local regulations and ensure your dog doesn't disturb the natural environment or other trail users.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in La Pernía?
A: The best time for running in La Pernía is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October) when the weather is milder and trails are clear of snow. Summer offers pleasant temperatures at higher altitudes, while spring and autumn provide vibrant foliage and fewer crowds. Winter running is possible but requires appropriate gear for snow and ice, especially on higher mountain trails.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging options in La Pernía?
A: For families, shorter and less challenging routes are recommended. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120125287/fossil-forest-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fossil Forest Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that might be suitable, offering an interesting natural experience without excessive difficulty or length. Always check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Is there wildlife to spot along the trails in La Pernía?
A: As part of the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, La Pernía is home to diverse wildlife. Runners might spot various bird species, deer, or even ibex in higher elevations. Early mornings or late afternoons are often the best times for wildlife viewing. Remember to observe from a distance and avoid disturbing animals.

Q: What should I wear or bring for running in La Pernía?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for changing mountain weather, it's wise to wear layers, even on warmer days. Trail running shoes with good grip are essential. Always carry water, a map or GPS device, and a fully charged phone. Depending on the season, consider a lightweight waterproof jacket and sun protection.

Q: Are there options for parking near the trailheads in La Pernía?
A: Many trailheads in and around La Pernía, especially those within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, typically offer designated parking areas. It's common for popular routes to have small car parks or roadside pull-offs nearby. Always check specific route information or local signage for parking availability.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in La Pernía?
A: The running routes in La Pernía are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the variety of terrain, and the opportunity to explore the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. The challenging ascents and scenic paths are frequently highlighted as key attractions.
tips:
The Piedrasluengas viewpoint is a perfect place to reach by bike. Located in the heart of the Cantabrian Mountains, in the Fuentes Carrionas and Fuente Cobre-Montaña de Palencia Natural Park, it rises to over 1,300 meters above sea level.
The views will leave you speechless. You'll see the Picos de Europa, Peña Labra, Tres Mares, Cuchillón, the Liébana Valley, and the Valdeprado Valley.

Limestone summit whose classic ascent leaves from the port of Piedrasluengas. This top offers panoramic views of the neighboring mountains such as the Peña Sagra or the Picos de Europa in the distance.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Peña Ciquera trail and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult and requires good to very good physical fitness. While much of the path is accessible, sure-footedness is essential, especially as you approach the summit. The final meters to the Peña Ciquera (1,636 m) peak involve exposed sections and some scrambling over a small gully, so it's not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail running route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, primarily hiking paths, with some sections on state roads. You'll traverse through a beautiful beech forest (hayedo) and open grassy areas (praderas), navigating different mountain features within the Cantabrian Mountains.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular panoramic views. From the starting point at the Piedrasluengas Viewpoint, you can see the Picos de Europa, Peña Labra, Tres Mares, Cuchillón, and the picturesque Liébana and Valdeprado Valleys. The summit of Peña Ciquera (1,636 m) provides some of the most impressive vistas of this sector of the Cantabrian mountain range.

faqs.question: Are there any historical features to see on the Peña Ciquera trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant portion of the route follows the "Camino de los Rojos" (Path of the Reds), a defensive line from the Spanish Civil War. You can observe remnants of trenches and machine gun posts that were used to control this crucial pass between Cantabria and Palencia.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run the Peña Ciquera trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed in various seasons, it's generally best to visit during spring, summer, or early autumn when the weather is more stable and the paths are clear of snow. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out, especially given the exposed sections near the summit.

faqs.question: Is the Peña Ciquera trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural Montaña Palentina generally allows dogs, but they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for exposed sections on this specific route.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: There are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for trail running on this route within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina. However, always respect natural park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Peña Ciquera trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the important Puerto de Piedrasluengas. You can find parking facilities near the Piedrasluengas Viewpoint, which serves as the trailhead for this route.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Transcantábrica, and briefly touches upon the [CIMA CA02] Piedrasluengas * Potes and [CIMA CA02] Piedrasluengas * Puentenansa routes.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a trail running trip on Peña Ciquera?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and exposed sections, it's crucial to bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water, snacks, a map or GPS device, and weather-appropriate clothing. A first-aid kit is also highly recommended due to the challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Is the Peña Ciquera trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This trail is described as a relatively short round trip, meaning it's a loop or a variation of a loop that brings you back to your starting point at the Puerto de Piedrasluengas.
